fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue company with a high forward P/E, but currently unprofitable with significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 44.8%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 441.7% — very high expected future growth.
Forward P/E 33.20 with PEG ratio 0.78 — growth may justify valuation.
Current ratio 3.83 and Quick ratio 2.85 — strong short-term liquidity.
Debt/Equity 25.36 — leverage is high but offset by strong liquidity.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — currently losing money.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — poor profitability.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-445.58M — significant cash burn.
Last quarter EPS surprise -44.8% — missed estimates significantly.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) is experiencing strong demand from data centers and AI computing, highlighted by a significant upgrade deal with Mediacom for one million homes. While this indicates robust growth prospects, the company's stock experienced a 9% tumble recently along with other optics stocks, suggesting some sector-wide cooling. The company's ability to meet this surging demand will be key, especially given its negative operating and profit margins.
